title: Steph Gilmore is back on tour, why now?
description: Eight-time world champion Steph Gilmore has decided to return to the WSL tour after two years away from the sport. She spent the time starting businesses with friends, playing a lot of guitar and even became a Barbie doll. What did she learn from her time away from surfing and how is she approaching her comeback?   Featured: Steph Gilmore, eight-time world champion surfer.    To catch up on everything that’s making sports headlines recently, listen to more episodes of ABC Sport Daily,’ hosted by Patrick Stack on ABC listen or wherever you get your podcasts, and get in touch with them on social media via @abc_sport. In the episodes we will cover big sporting personalities and all sports, including cricket, soccer, F1, NBA, AFL, AFLW to NRLW & NRL news, to covering competitions like the Olympics, the World Cup, The Ashes, Grand Prix and Grand Finals and more.    Subscribe to the ABC Sport Newsletter (https://www.abc.net.au/connect)

title: Labor wins South Australian election by a landslide | ABC NEWS
description: The Labor government has claimed a second straight term after comfortably winning the South Australian election.  The state's Premier Peter Malinauskas emphasised the importance of kindness in his victory speech after he received concession calls from Liberal Leader Ashton Hurn and One Nation counterpart Cory Bernardi. title: Cyclone Narelle lashes Northern Territory with heavy winds and rain | ABC NEWS
description: Northern Territory residents are being urged to take shelter after Tropical Cyclone Narelle crossed the territory’s east coast near Cape Shield.  The storm re-intensified in the Gulf of Carpentaria after battering Far North Queensland with destructive winds and flooding. The system is expected to compound pressure on the Katherine and Daly River communities after recent floods devastated the region. description: London has confirmed an unsuccessful Iranian strike on a UK-US naval base in the Indian Ocean. title: Former FBI boss Robert Mueller dies aged 81 | ABC NEWS
description: Former FBI director Robert Mueller has died at age 81.  He was credited for transforming the US federal law enforcement agency into a counter-terrorism force in the wake of the September 11 attacks and bringing down the murder rate in Washington. US journalist Jay Newton-Small says he will unfortunately be remembered for a ‘frivolous’ investigation into US President Donald Trump’s links to alleged Russian political interference despite his unwavering commitment to the rule of law as the head of the FBI. title: Chris Bowen on fuel shipments being cancelled | Insiders | ABC NEWS
description: Energy Minister Chris Bowen says six fuel tankers that were scheduled to deliver oil to Australia next month were recently cancelled or deferred, out of about 81 ships expected from mid-April to mid-May.  Mr Bowen said there would be "bumps" in supply, but he hoped that gave Australians a sense of the scale of impact he expected the Middle East conflict to have on Australia.  He said that while the government had contingencies for fuel shortages, Australia was "a long way" from fuel rationing.  The minister urged Australians to buy only the fuel they needed, saying supply issues in isolated spots in the regions had been driven purely by a spike in demand from customers that "any" supply chain would struggle to manage.  Australia's fuel reserves contain 38 days' worth of petrol, and 30 days each of diesel and jet fuel, with fresh supplies continuing to arrive in Australia. title: Six fuel ships to Australia cancelled or deferred, though gap filled | ABC NEWS
description: Energy Minister Chris Bowen says some fuel shipments to Australia have been cancelled or delayed due to the war in the Middle East, warning of potential supply disruptions. The conflict has entered its fourth week, and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, causing a massive disruption to the global oil trade and sending petrol and diesel prices soaring. Mr Bowen also said while the government had contingencies for fuel shortages, Australia was "a long way" from fuel rationing. title: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle hits NT, severe flood risk | ABC NEWS
description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle, now a tropical low, threatens communities as it travels rapidly across the recently-flood struck Katherine region in the Northern Territory towards Western Australia. NT Chief Minister Lia Finocchiaro urgently warned residents to stay alert, as the Bureau of Meteorology issued a major flood warning for Nitmiluk Centre overnight and for Katherine Bridge from Monday afternoon.
